preparation_method: soy sauce and ginger garnish typical in Zi Char style

serving.description: 1 whole fish (~1 kg as served at restaurant)
serving.natural_language_portion: One whole red garoupa of approximately 1 kg, typically shared among 3–5 diners as part of a Zi Char or seafood meal.

nutrient_source.title: Grouper, cooked, dry heat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 15033)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for grouper (cooked, dry heat) used as the closest approved source for red garoupa (Epinephelus spp.). Red garoupa is a grouper species; nutrient profile is closely comparable. Values represent plain cooked fish without sauce additions. Sodium adjusted upward to reflect typical Zi Char soy-sauce preparation (~300 mg/100g estimated); base fish sodium from USDA is ~53 mg/100g. Nutrient values are per 100g edible cooked flesh.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fish and seafood — analogous estimate based on protein-dominant, zero-carbohydrate marine fish
gi_source.source_match_notes: Red garoupa contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100g (cooked flesh). No direct GI measurement exists for red garoupa in the University of Sydney GI Database. By established convention for protein-dominant, carbohydrate-free fish and seafood, GI is assigned as 5 (effectively negligible), gi_category low. Glycemic load per 1000g serving = 0.0 as carbohydrate content is 0g. This approach is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database treatment of non-carbohydrate foods.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce preparation
hypertension.guidance: Red garoupa as plain steamed fish is naturally low in sodium (~53 mg/100g), but Zi Char-style preparation with soy sauce significantly increases sodium to approximately 300 mg/100g; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-soy-sauce preparation or limit portion size to manage sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate content and high-quality lean protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: Red garoupa contains no carbohydrates and has a negligible glycemic index, making it an excellent protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; it supports glycemic control and satiety without raising postprandial blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Lean protein with low saturated fat and beneficial omega-3 fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Red garoupa is a lean fish with low saturated fat (0.3 g/100g) and contains polyunsaturated fatty acids including omega-3s, which are cardioprotective; steamed preparation is preferred over fried to maintain this cardiovascular benefit, though soy sauce sodium should be monitored.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Red Garoupa (红斑鱼, Kerapu Merah) is a prized marine fish belonging to the grouper family (Epinephelidae), widely consumed across Singapore, Malaysia, and the broader Southeast Asian region. In Singaporean and Malaysian Zi Char cuisine, red garoupa is most commonly prepared as a whole steamed fish, typically garnished with julienned ginger, spring onion, and coriander, then finished with a pour of hot sesame oil and light soy sauce. The flesh is firm yet tender, mildly sweet, and delicately flavoured with a clean oceanic taste that is highly regarded in Chinese seafood dining culture. Red garoupa is a premium restaurant fish; a whole 1 kg fish is typically priced at SGD 80–120 and is shared among 3–5 diners. Nutritionally, red garoupa is an excellent source of lean, high-quality protein (~24.8 g/100g) with very low fat content (~1.3 g/100g) and zero carbohydrates, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate, high-protein dietary patterns. It provides meaningful amounts of potassium (483 mg/100g), phosphorus (272 mg/100g), selenium (36.5 mcg/100g), and vitamin B12 (0.6 mcg/100g). The glycemic index is effectively zero due to the absence of carbohydrates, making it ideal for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The primary clinical concern is sodium from soy sauce in Zi Char preparation. Red garoupa is also known as red grouper, Hong Ban (红斑), and is classified under the Epinephelus genus. It is halal-certified in most contexts and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: How many calories in steamed red garoupa?
Is red garoupa good for diabetics?
Is red garoupa high in protein?
Red garoupa vs red snapper nutrition
What fish is used in Zi Char steamed fish?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy grouper (cooked, dry heat), which is the closest approved source for red garoupa as a grouper species. No direct HPB Singapore or IMR Malaysia MyFCD entry for red garoupa was identified. Sodium has been adjusted upward to reflect typical Zi Char soy sauce preparation. GI is an analogous estimate based on zero carbohydrate content. Confidence is medium rather than high due to source adaptation rather than exact match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100g cooked edible flesh, adapted from USDA SR Legacy grouper (NDB 15033 / FDC 175159). Red garoupa is a grouper species (Epinephelus spp.) and the USDA grouper entry is the closest approved source. 2. Sodium_mg is set to 300.0 mg/100g as an estimated value reflecting typical Zi Char soy sauce preparation; base fish sodium from USDA is approximately 53 mg/100g. 3. carbohydrate_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for cooked grouper flesh; grouper is a protein-dominant fish with no carbohydrate content. sugar_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as fish contains no dietary fibre. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for cooked grouper. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy grouper entry used. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy grouper entry used. 5. GI is set to 5 (effectively negligible) as this is a carbohydrate-free fish; gi_category low per convention for protein-dominant, zero-carbohydrate marine fish. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. 6. The input record listed cuisine_tags including 'Crab' which appears to be a field survey platform tag error; red garoupa is a fish, not a crab. This tag has been disregarded. 7. Price of SGD 120 for 1 kg whole fish is consistent with premium live seafood pricing at Singapore seafood restaurants as of 2024–2026. 8. FoodOn URI FOODON_03411433 and SNOMED CT 226888009 are used as closest_parent matches; no exact ontology code for red garoupa species confirmed. FoodEx2 A00LC covers the grouper/seabass commodity group.
